one_time_description: Legal fees of $4,047,864 owing related to Comprehensive Settlement Agreement (CSA) for flooding on the First Nation, not yet recorded in financial statements pending finalization of CSA in 2023-24
economic_notes: Nation is economically dependent on Indigenous Services Canada funding under Treaties administered by ISC under the Indian Act. Comprehensive Settlement Agreement being negotiated with Canada and Manitoba related to flooding.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024). CMHC Housing Authority is the one consolidated subsidiary. A Comprehensive Settlement Agreement related to flooding between the First Nation, Canada, and Manitoba was agreed to in principle but not finalized; legal fees of $4,047,864 are not recorded in these statements. No schedule of remuneration for Chief, Council or staff was included in the document. Net debt position at year end is -$517,320. Significant capital additions of $15,286,792 in tangible capital assets (primarily band buildings $13,619,080 and heavy equipment $1,470,134). OSR calculated as total revenue $9,547,606 less ISC $8,995,823 less CMHC $85,048 = $466,735.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
High ISC dependency (94.22%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
